mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances following a >0.2pp unemployment jump, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when isolated to this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity volatility and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL remains above 3.5%, or the threshold fails to improve out-of-sample classification accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP during a late-cycle expansion signals economic deceleration, prompting equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of historical late-cycle instances, or the signal predictive accuracy does not exceed random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum, which correlates with short-term equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative direction in >55% of observations following positive industrial production growth, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.
